verdict.summary: The change correctly consults the lease-release result in both unsafe-claim paths and fails loud when release cannot be confirmed: it records a lane error, emits a dedicated coarse defer reason, and does not falsely report recovery. Successful releases retain the prior defer behavior. The added regression test is non-vacuous because it exercises both boolean outcomes and asserts the release attempt, launch count, dedicated reason, and lane-error flip; the failure-side assertions would fail against the old implementation. No trust check is weakened, no permissive fallback is introduced, and the new logging remains free of account handles and tokens.
